nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tyson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Old French tison ‘firebrand’, perhaps for someone with a fiery temperament.
Americanized form of German   Theissen   or   Theisen  .

variants.name: Dye
meanings: English (Norfolk): from the Middle English female personal name Dye, a pet form of Denis(e) (from Latin Dionisia); see Dennis .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Tyson Lord of Alnwick was born in 1050, in Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick, was 15 and his mother, Beatrix of Normandy, was 12. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1096, in Malton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Tyson Lord of Alnwick est né en 1050, à Alnwick Castle, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Beatrix of Normandy, avait 12 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1096, à Malton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Tyson Lord of Alnwick(이)가 1050년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 알윅 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert de Tesson Lord of Alnwick(은)는 15세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrix of Normandy(은)는 12세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1096년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 맬턴에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.
